platformMix.note: Viewer share uses GA4 active users grouped from stream and device data. People who use more than one platform may appear in more than one bucket. Amazon/Fire TV is inferred from Amazon AFT smart-TV device models until the developer confirms a cleaner Fire TV label.

ElectricNOW iPhone, iPad, and Apple TV app activity.

plainEnglishInsights: Overall, ElectricNOW's latest complete week (May 25-May 31, 2026) shows a platform audience of 7.8K GA4 active users (excluding landing-page acquisition), up +1.1% from the prior week, while engagement softened: engaged sessions were -1.9% and the engagement rate eased from 81.8% to 80.2%. The short version: paid ads are buying real installs efficiently, audience is growing modestly, but the depth of viewing those new users do still needs to be proven.

Paid acquisition - simple read: the ads look promising and cost per install (CPI) is acceptable. Meta spent $3,146.72 from May 24-May 30, 2026 and drove 3,259 app downloads at about $0.97 blended CPI. Android / Google Play is cheaper than iOS ($0.80 Android vs $1.23 iOS), so the cheapest volume is coming from Android.

Google Ads is STALE this run: the connector was rate-limited on June 1, so we are showing the last successful pull (PCSM ElectricNow App Promotion - Android, $241.08 from May 12-May 26 at about $0.54 per Android first-open). These numbers are preserved, not zeroed, and should be refreshed once the connector recovers.

Bottom line on ads: paid spend is producing installs at reasonable cost, and platform audience grew modestly week over week, but engaged sessions and the engagement rate both softened. So the ads are clearly buying installs - the open question is whether those installs turn into deep, repeat viewing. We still need downstream proof of viewing depth.

Measured ElectricNOW platform audience was 7.8K GA4 active users excluding landing-page acquisition paths, +1.1% vs the previous week. Landing-page traffic is tracked separately as acquisition only and is not counted as platform audience.

Property-wide GA4 active users (including landing-page acquisition) were 7.8K this week versus 7.7K the prior week; this property-wide number is for acquisition context and is not the platform-audience figure above.

Roku app engagement (May 24-May 30 report, generated June 1): 267 new installs, 130 uninstalls, 288,133 cumulative installs to date, 269 average daily viewers, 88.13 average minutes per viewer, and 2,769 total hours streamed.

Visible platform sales (audited) combine Stripe, Roku, and the manual Apple App Store snapshot: 43 purchases and $806.57 year to date - Stripe 21/$439.79, Roku 14/$206.86 ($165.46 developer revenue share), and Apple 8/$159.92 ($111.94 net). This is still not total ElectricNOW sales because Google Play is handled separately and Apple is a manual snapshot.

Top watched on-demand programs in GA4 this week include The Ark, Leverage, Almost Paradise, Leverage: Redemption, The Librarians. Live / FAST / linear channels are excluded from these on-demand lists and tracked separately as Live channel usage.

Next tests to prove downstream viewing depth: (1) Cohort paid installs from Meta (Android vs iOS) and measure 7-day return rate and minutes watched per install, not just install count. (2) Shift a slice of Meta budget toward the cheaper Android / Google Play campaigns and watch whether engaged sessions and watch time hold up. (3) Stand up a clean install-to-first-play funnel in GA4 so we can see how many paid installs reach an actual video play. (4) Once the Google Ads connector recovers, compare Google Android first-open cost and downstream viewing against Meta Android. (5) Add a simple weekly view of new-install viewing minutes so the team can tell, at a glance, whether paid users are becoming real viewers.

GA4, Stripe, Roku, and the manual Apple App Store snapshot should still be reconciled with Google Play, Amagi, ad-server, YouTube, Meta, and final app-store reporting before making final business decisions.

live.note: Live usage is based on GA4 screen name 'Live' plus playback events that also carried the Live screen name.

onDemand.note: On Demand usage is based on GA4 screen name 'On Demand'. Title-level viewing is shown below from GA4 video_title play events.

titleViewership.note: Top programs roll up fragmented GA4 play-event labels into parent shows/collections where possible, then use a GA4 filter to estimate deduped active users for each program. Play events remain the ranking metric because ads monetize playback; active users are total GA4 users who triggered matching play events for that program during the period. On-demand shows and collections only — live/FAST/linear channels (DARK MATTER TV, Kung Fu Movies, spot on news, ToonOvation, ElectricNOW en Espanol, Stoner TV Network, etc.) are excluded here and reported separately under Live channel usage. Top 50 may list fewer than 50 entries until the next GA4 refresh overfetches with the stricter row-level predicate. Treat this as GA-tracked viewership, not a fully cleaned catalog report yet, until the app sends content_type, series_title, season, episode, and video_id consistently.
titleViewership.topVideoTitlesNote: Top videos shows individual on-demand video plays only. Live/FAST/linear channel rows (e.g. DARK MATTER TV, Kung Fu Movies, spot on news, ToonOvation, ElectricNOW en Espanol, Stoner TV Network) are excluded here and reported separately in 'Live channel usage'. Top 50 may list fewer than 50 entries until the next GA4 refresh overfetches with the stricter row-level live-channel predicate.

metaCampaignContext.interpretation: Judge the newest phase by installs, app opens, viewing, Live usage, purchases, and ad value rather than by landing-page traffic.

manualAppleSalesUpdate.note: Preserved May 25 Apple sale (Purchase HD 15, $14.99 gross). 4 manual entries: May 13, May 18 (2), May 25.

googleAdsSummary.plainEnglish: Google Ads is now useful for judging paid Android app-campaign efficiency directly: it shows spend, clicks, installs/first-opens, conversion rate, and cost per conversion. This should sit beside GA4 viewing quality so we can see not only whether ads are buying installs, but whether those installs become viewers.
googleAdsSummary.dataFreshnessNote: STALE THIS RUN: Google Ads connector rate-limited on 2026-06-01; preserving last successful campaign totals through May 26, 2026. Device and conversion-action breakouts are from the prior detailed segment pull.
googleAdsSummary.statusDetail: Google Ads connector was rate-limited on the 2026-06-01 refresh (RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, retry delay ~228s). Showing the last successful connector pull through May 26, 2026. Figures are not zeroed; they are the most recent good data and should be treated as stale until the next successful pull.

googleAds.note: Paid acquisition looks efficient on cost, but the growth signal is mixed: Google Ads spent $241.08 from May 12-May 26, 2026, generated 46,962 paid-campaign impressions, 1,458 clicks, and 443 Android first-open conversions at about $0.54 per first open. That cost per first open is strong for app acquisition, but platform audience was down 8.0% and viewing was down 2.7% in the latest GA4 week, so the ads appear good at buying installs/opens but we still need to watch whether those paid users convert into sustained viewers and time spent. Meta remains the bigger spend driver in the latest Nathan report (May 24-May 30) at $3,146.72 for 3,259 downloads (about $0.97 blended cost per download), with Android still cheaper than iOS; iOS cost per download improved to $1.23 while Android rose to $0.80, and impressions/link clicks softened (down 4.8% and 13.1%). The next question for Patrick/Nathan is whether the lower-cost Android volume produces real viewing quality.
googleAds.staleNote: Google Ads connector rate-limited on 2026-06-01; last successful pull May 12-May 26, 2026. Values preserved (not zeroed) and marked stale.

metaAdsSummary.emailSubject: ElectricNow | Meta Ads Performance Dashboard Summary (2026-05-24 to 2026-05-30)
metaAdsSummary.reportCadence: Twice weekly (Sundays and Thursdays at 6am PST/PDT), each report covers Last 7 days

campaigns.name: PCSM - ElectricNow App - Google Play - Women

metaAdsSummary.plainEnglish: This is Meta paid acquisition data from Nathan / Forge One (pcsocialmedia.com). It covers Meta ad campaigns running on Facebook and Instagram that drive iOS and Android app installs for ElectricNOW. It is a separate paid channel from Google Ads (which is pulled via connector and shown above) and should be read alongside it, not added on top in a way that double-counts. These are Meta-platform ad impressions and clicks, NOT in-app ad impressions served inside the ElectricNOW app — those live in the Ad event signals panel.
metaAdsSummary.paidAcquisitionInterpretation: Meta paid acquisition this week (May 24-May 30) looks solid but mixed. On roughly flat spend of $3,146.72, Meta delivered 3,259 app downloads at about $0.97 blended cost per download — efficient for app acquisition and steady week-over-week. iOS efficiency improved: 1,280 iOS downloads at $1.23 each (cost down 7.9%), while Android worsened a bit: 1,979 downloads at $0.80 each (cost up 14.2%, volume down 12.6%); Android still buys cheaper installs than iOS overall. Upper-funnel signals softened — impressions fell 4.8% to 250,366 and link clicks fell 13.1% to 8,057, pushing cost/click up 11.7% to $0.38 — yet installs/downloads stayed substantial, so the campaigns are still converting clicks to installs effectively. Net read: paid ads remain effective on a cost-per-install basis and are not concerning, but the falling impressions/clicks are worth watching. Important caveat: this report only proves installs, not viewing quality — we still need GA4 (property 497892271) downstream data to judge whether these Meta-driven installs become real viewers and time spent.
metaAdsSummary.doubleCountingNote: Use only Meta rows from Nathan's reports. Nathan also sends a blended Meta + Google Ads report; the Google Ads rows in that blended report are NOT used here because Google Ads is already pulled directly from the Google Ads connector. The Meta numbers shown here are independent of the Google Ads connector totals.
metaAdsSummary.inAppAdsDistinction: Meta campaign impressions (250,366 this period) are paid ad placements on Meta’s own platforms (Facebook/Instagram feeds, stories, etc.) and are NOT the same as ElectricNOW in-app ad impressions counted in the Ad event signals panel.

priorPeriod.emailSubject: ElectricNow | Meta Ads Performance Dashboard Summary (2026-05-17 to 2026-05-23)

metaAdsSummary.dedupeNote: May 24-May 30 report integrated from authoritative nathan@pcsocialmedia.com email (2026-05-31T13:31:02Z). Forwarded/duplicate copies from nathan@forgeonemarketing.com for the same period (and a May 21-May 27 forward) were intentionally NOT counted.

ga4PurchaseSignals.sourceDetail: These are GA4 in_app_purchase events from property 497892271. They are directional purchase signals only — GA4 does not return Google Play / Android billing transaction IDs, so these rows must be reconciled against Google Play Console before being treated as financial revenue. They are intentionally kept out of the Stripe / Roku / Apple visible-sales rollup above.

ga4PurchaseSignals.reconciliationDetail: Likely Google Play / Android billing. Reconcile against Google Play Console before counting toward final sales. Do not double count if the same purchase later appears in Stripe, Roku, Apple, or official Google Play reporting.

ga4PurchaseSignals.note: GA4 detected $29.98 of Android in-app purchase activity on May 21, 2026 across 2 in_app_purchase events (1 Android mobile in Philadelphia at appVersion 16.019; 1 Android smart TV in Vancouver, WA at appVersion 16.026). This is most likely Google Play / Android billing. It is shown separately from Stripe, Roku, and the manual Apple App Store snapshot and is NOT included in visible platform sales until it is reconciled with Google Play Console.
